description:
A capacity-planning team is preparing a lightweight dashboard that only needs the
    most important “used” figures from the full cluster telemetry log.  
    The raw tab-separated telemetry file is already present on the system at  

        /home/user/data/cluster_resource_usage.tsv

    1. Review the contents of the file.  
       • It is **TAB-delimited** (Unicode 0x09).  
       • The header row reads exactly:  
         Timestamp<TAB>Node<TAB>CPU_Used<TAB>CPU_Total<TAB>Memory_Used<TAB>Memory_Total<TAB>Disk_Used<TAB>Disk_Total  

    2. Produce a trimmed, comma-separated summary that keeps only the following
       columns and in this precise order:  

         Timestamp, CPU_Used, Memory_Used, Disk_Used

       Notes about the required output format:  
       • The new file must be called  

             /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.csv  

       • It must be **comma-separated with no whitespace** after the commas.  
       • The first line must be an exact header matching the four retained column
         names separated by **commas** (not tabs, not spaces):  
         Timestamp,CPU_Used,Memory_Used,Disk_Used  
       • Each data line must preserve the original row order from the source file.  
       • There must be exactly 5 data rows beneath the header (total of 6 lines).  
       • No blank lines are allowed at the beginning or end of the file.  
       • Only ASCII digits, letters, ISO-8601 timestamp, and commas may appear
         (i.e., no stray tab or space characters).

    3. After creating the CSV, generate a one-line confirmation log at  

           /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.log  

       The line must read **verbatim** (including the period):  
           SUCCESS: 6 lines written to /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.csv.

    Directory /home/user/output may not exist yet, so be sure to create it first.
    Achieve the whole transformation with a single compound command (a single
    line entered in the terminal) that uses standard GNU text-processing tools
    (e.g., cut, tr, etc.) without invoking any external scripting language.

    The automated test will check:  
       • Presence and exact content of /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.csv.  
       • Presence and exact single-line content of /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.log.  
       • That the trimmed CSV contains exactly 6 lines and the four requested
         columns, in the correct order, with comma separators and no extra
         whitespace.
truth:
Initial file: /home/user/data/cluster_resource_usage.tsv  
    Exact byte content (TABs shown as \t for clarity—actual file contains raw 0x09 bytes):

        Timestamp\tNode\tCPU_Used\tCPU_Total\tMemory_Used\tMemory_Total\tDisk_Used\tDisk_Total
        2023-08-07T00:00:00Z\tnode01\t45\t64\t32768\t65536\t120\t250
        2023-08-07T00:00:00Z\tnode02\t53\t64\t41984\t65536\t140\t250
        2023-08-07T01:00:00Z\tnode01\t47\t64\t33000\t65536\t121\t250
        2023-08-07T01:00:00Z\tnode02\t52\t64\t42050\t65536\t141\t250
        2023-08-07T02:00:00Z\tnode01\t49\t64\t33100\t65536\t122\t250

    Expected file created by the agent: /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.csv  
    Exact required content:

        Timestamp,CPU_Used,Memory_Used,Disk_Used
        2023-08-07T00:00:00Z,45,32768,120
        2023-08-07T00:00:00Z,53,41984,140
        2023-08-07T01:00:00Z,47,33000,121
        2023-08-07T01:00:00Z,52,42050,141
        2023-08-07T02:00:00Z,49,33100,122

    Expected log file: /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.log  
    Exact required content (including trailing period, no newline required by test but OK if present):

        SUCCESS: 6 lines written to /home/user/output/usage_trimmed.csv.

    Permissions:  
       • /home/user/data and its files already exist and are readable.  
       • The agent must create /home/user/output with mode 0755 (default mkdir).  
       • The agent must create both output files with default umask-controlled
         permissions (0644 or similar) so they are readable by the test harness.
